content.content:
1→# Requirements: OpenTelemetry Observability Migration
     2→
     3→## Feature Overview
     4→
     5→Replace Pino structured logging with OpenTelemetry distributed tracing and metrics across the Brain server. Enable Vercel AI SDK `experimental_telemetry` on all LLM calls. Provide developer/operator visibility into LLM behavior, request lifecycle, operational health, and token costs.
     6→
     7→## Scope
     8→
     9→**In scope**:
    10→- OTEL SDK setup with trace and metric providers
    11→- Console exporter (dev) and OTLP exporter (prod)
    12→- AI SDK `experimental_telemetry` on all `generateObject`, `streamText`, and `ToolLoopAgent` calls
    13→- HTTP request tracing (root spans replacing `withRequestLogging`)
    14→- Pipeline-stage child spans (extraction, chat agent, tool calls, subagents)
    15→- OTEL metrics (LLM duration, token counters, HTTP request metrics)
    16→- Pino removal and replacement with thin console logger for startup
    17→- Removal of `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` event functions
    18→
    19→**Out of scope**:
    20→- SurrealDB graph traces (application-level audit trail -- stays as-is)
    21→- Trace/metrics backend infrastructure (Jaeger, Grafana, etc.)
    22→- Dashboard creation (downstream of OTLP export)
    23→- Alerting rules
    24→- Compliance/audit requirements beyond existing graph traces
    25→
    26→## Constraints
    27→
    28→- Must work on Bun runtime (not just Node.js)
    29→- Must coexist with existing `AsyncLocalStorage`-based `requestContext`
    30→- Console exporter must work with zero infrastructure in dev
    31→- OTLP exporter activation controlled by standard OTEL env vars only
    32→- No application code should reference specific telemetry backends
    33→- SurrealDB graph traces must remain completely independent
    34→- Telemetry must not expose raw prompt content in spans by default
    35→
    36→## Dependencies
    37→
    38→- `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` (or equivalent Bun-compatible OTEL SDK)
    39→- `@opentelemetry/api`
    40→- `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node`
    41→- `@opentelemetry/sdk-metrics`
    42→- `@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http`
    43→- `@opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-http`
    44→- Vercel AI SDK `experimental_telemetry` API (already available in installed version)
    45→
    46→## Non-Functional Requirements
    47→
    48→### Performance
    49→- OTEL instrumentation must add less than 5ms overhead per request
    50→- Metric recording must be non-blocking
    51→- Span export must be asynchronous and buffered (never block request handling)
    52→
    53→### Reliability
    54→- OTEL SDK failure must not crash the server
    55→- Unreachable OTLP endpoint must not affect request processing
    56→- Graceful degradation: if OTEL SDK fails to initialize, spans become no-ops
    57→
    58→### Compatibility
    59→- Must work with Bun >= 1.3
    60→- Must support standard OTEL environment variables per specification
    61→- Must not conflict with `@ai-sdk/devtools` middleware (currently wrapping all models)
    62→
    63→## Function ID Taxonomy
    64→
    65→Every AI SDK call must include a `functionId` from this taxonomy:
    66→
    67→| Function ID | Call Site | Description |
    68→|---|---|---|
    69→| `brain.extraction.generate` | extraction pipeline | Entity extraction from messages |
    70→| `brain.extraction.dedupe` | extraction pipeline | Deduplication scoring |
    71→| `brain.chat.agent` | chat handler | Chat agent tool-use responses |
    72→| `brain.chat.stream` | chat route | Streaming chat responses |
    73→| `brain.pm.agent` | PM subagent | Work planning and organization |
    74→| `brain.observer.verify` | observer | Observation verification |
    75→| `brain.observer.peer-review` | observer | Peer review cross-validation |
    76→| `brain.behavior.score` | behavior scorer | Behavior scoring |
    77→| `brain.onboarding.generate` | onboarding | Onboarding responses |
    78→| `brain.intent.authorize` | intent auth | Intent authorization |
    79→| `brain.analytics.agent` | analytics agent | Analytics queries |
    80→
    81→## Metrics Definitions
    82→
    83→| Metric Name | Type | Unit | Attributes | Purpose |
    84→|---|---|---|---|---|
    85→| `brain.llm.duration` | Histogram | ms | functionId, model | LLM call latency distribution |
    86→| `brain.llm.tokens.prompt` | Counter | tokens | functionId, model | Prompt token usage |
    87→| `brain.llm.tokens.completion` | Counter | tokens | functionId, model | Completion token usage |
    88→| `brain.llm.errors` | Counter | count | functionId, model, error_type | LLM call failures |
    89→| `brain.http.duration` | Histogram | ms | method, route, status_code | HTTP request latency |
    90→| `brain.http.requests` | Counter | count | method, route, status_code | HTTP request volume |
    91→| `brain.extraction.entities` | Counter | count | entity_type | Extracted entity volume |
    92→
    93→## Migration Strategy
    94→
    95→### What Gets Removed
    96→1. `pino` dependency (v10.3.1)
    97→2. `app/src/server/logging.ts` -- Pino configuration
    98→3. `logInfo`, `logWarn`, `logError`, `logDebug` from `app/src/server/http/observability.ts`
    99→4. `withRequestLogging` from `app/src/server/http/request-logging.ts`
   100→5. All `logInfo("event.name", { data })` call sites across the codebase
   101→
   102→### What Gets Added
   103→1. `app/src/server/telemetry/init.ts` -- OTEL provider initialization
   104→2. `app/src/server/telemetry/ai-telemetry.ts` -- AI SDK telemetry config factory
   105→3. `app/src/server/telemetry/metrics.ts` -- metric instrument definitions
   106→4. `app/src/server/telemetry/function-ids.ts` -- function ID constants
   107→5. HTTP request tracing wrapper (replaces `withRequestLogging`)
   108→6. Thin console logger for startup messages
   109→
   110→### What Stays
   111→1. `app/src/server/request-context.ts` -- `AsyncLocalStorage` (coexists with OTEL context)
   112→2. `app/src/server/http/errors.ts` -- error primitives (unchanged)
   113→3. `elapsedMs()` from observability.ts -- may be retained for non-span timing
   114→4. SurrealDB graph traces -- completely independent, not touched
   115→5. `@ai-sdk/devtools` middleware -- must coexist with `experimental_telemetry`
   116→
   117→## Traceability Matrix
   118→
   119→| Requirement Area | Jobs Served |
   120→|---|---|
   121→| AI SDK telemetry on all LLM calls | Job 1 (Debug LLM), Job 4 (Cost Visibility) |
   122→| HTTP request root spans | Job 2 (Request Tracing), Job 3 (Monitoring) |
   123→| Pipeline child spans | Job 2 (Request Tracing), Job 1 (Debug LLM) |
   124→| LLM metrics (duration, tokens, errors) | Job 3 (Monitoring), Job 4 (Cost Visibility) |
   125→| HTTP metrics | Job 3 (Monitoring) |
   126→| Console exporter for dev | Job 1 (Debug LLM), Job 2 (Request Tracing) |
   127→| OTLP exporter for prod | Job 3 (Monitoring), Job 4 (Cost Visibility) |
   128→| Function ID taxonomy | Job 1 (Debug LLM), Job 4 (Cost Visibility) |
   129→| Pino removal | All jobs (migration prerequisite) |
   130→

1→# User Stories: OpenTelemetry Observability Migration
     2→
     3→---
     4→
     5→## US-OT01: OTEL SDK Bootstrap
     6→
     7→### Problem
     8→Marcus is the sole operator of the Brain platform. He wants to add distributed tracing and metrics but currently has no OpenTelemetry infrastructure. He needs the SDK initialized at startup with zero-config dev experience (console exporter) and standard OTLP export for production -- without the SDK failure ever crashing the server.
     9→
    10→### Who
    11→- Developer/Operator | Running Brain locally and in production | Needs observable telemetry from day one of migration
    12→
    13→### Solution
    14→Initialize OpenTelemetry TracerProvider and MeterProvider at server startup, before `Bun.serve()`. Console exporter active by default. OTLP exporter activates when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set. Graceful degradation if SDK fails.
    15→
    16→### Jobs Served
    17→- Job 1 (Debug LLM Calls) -- prerequisite
    18→- Job 2 (Request Tracing) -- prerequisite
    19→- Job 3 (Operational Monitoring) -- prerequisite
    20→- Job 4 (Cost Visibility) -- prerequisite
    21→
    22→### Domain Examples
    23→#### 1: Dev startup with console exporter
    24→Marcus runs `bun run dev` without any OTEL env vars set. The server starts, TracerProvider initializes with ConsoleSpanExporter, and he sees span output in the terminal when the first request arrives.
    25→
    26→#### 2: Prod startup with OTLP exporter
    27→Marcus sets `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://collector:4318` and `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=brain`. The server starts, TracerProvider initializes with OTLPTraceExporter, spans are sent to the collector. Console exporter is not active.
    28→
    29→#### 3: SDK initialization failure
    30→Marcus upgrades Bun to a version with a breaking change in the async hooks API. The OTEL SDK fails to initialize. The server starts anyway, all tracer/meter calls return no-op instances, and a startup warning is logged to console.
    31→
    32→### UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    33→
    34→#### Scenario: Console exporter active in dev
    35→```
    36→Given Marcus starts the Brain server with no OTEL environment variables set
    37→When the first HTTP request is processed
    38→Then span data for the request appears in the terminal console output
    39→And the span includes service.name "brain"
    40→```
    41→
    42→#### Scenario: OTLP exporter active in prod
    43→```
    44→Given Marcus has set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to "http://collector:4318"
    45→And Marcus has set OTEL_SERVICE_NAME to "brain"
    46→When the Brain server starts
    47→Then the TracerProvider is configured with OTLPTraceExporter
    48→And spans are exported to the configured endpoint
    49→And no spans appear in the console
    50→```
    51→
    52→#### Scenario: Graceful degradation on SDK failure
    53→```
    54→Given the OTEL SDK fails to initialize due to a runtime incompatibility
    55→When the Brain server starts
    56→Then the server starts successfully without crashing
    57→And a warning message appears in the console: "OTEL SDK failed to initialize, telemetry disabled"
    58→And all subsequent tracer calls return no-op spans
    59→```
    60→
    61→#### Scenario: Metrics provider initializes alongside traces
    62→```
    63→Given Marcus starts the Brain server with default configuration
    64→When the server has started
    65→Then both TracerProvider and MeterProvider are active
    66→And metric instruments can be created without errors
    67→```
    68→
    69→### Acceptance Criteria
    70→- [ ] TracerProvider and MeterProvider initialize before `Bun.serve()` starts
    71→- [ ] Console exporter is the default when no OTLP endpoint is configured
    72→- [ ] OTLP exporter activates only when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set
    73→- [ ] SDK initialization failure does not prevent server startup
    74→- [ ] Service name defaults to "brain" and is overridable via `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`
    75→
    76→### Technical Notes
    77→- OTEL SDK must be compatible with Bun runtime (test during spike if needed)
    78→- Initialization must run before any route handlers are registered
    79→- Standard OTEL env vars per spec: `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`
    80→
    81→### Dependencies
    82→- None (first story in the chain)
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→## US-OT02: Pino Removal & Console Logger
    87→
    88→### Problem
    89→Marcus currently has Pino (v10.3.1) as the structured logging library with `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` wrappers used across the codebase. With OTEL replacing structured event logging via spans, Pino becomes redundant. However, startup messages (before OTEL initializes) and non-request-scoped messages still need a simple output path.
    90→
    91→### Who
    92→- Developer/Operator | Maintaining the codebase | Needs clean migration without losing startup visibility
    93→
    94→### Solution
    95→Remove Pino dependency and all `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` call sites. Replace with a thin console-based logger for startup messages only. All request-scoped logging moves to OTEL spans.
    96→
    97→### Jobs Served
    98→- All jobs (migration prerequisite -- removes the old system)
    99→
   100→### Domain Examples
   101→#### 1: Startup message migration
   102→`logInfo("server.started", { port: 3000 })` becomes `console.log("[brain] Server started on port 3000")`. Simple, no structured JSON needed for startup.
   103→
   104→#### 2: Request-scoped event migration
   105→`logInfo("extraction.generate.completed", { durationMs: 2341, entityCount: 5 })` becomes a span event on the extraction span: `span.addEvent("extraction.generate.completed", { entityCount: 5 })` with duration captured by the span itself.
   106→
   107→#### 3: Error logging migration
   108→`logError("observer.llm.error", error, { functionId: "verify" })` becomes `span.recordException(error)` with `span.setStatus({ code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR })`.
   109→
   110→### UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   111→
   112→#### Scenario: Startup messages appear without Pino
   113→```
   114→Given Pino has been removed from the project dependencies
   115→When Marcus starts the Brain server with bun run dev
   116→Then startup messages (port, database connection, migration status) appear in the console
   117→And no Pino-formatted JSON lines appear in the output
   118→```
   119→
   120→#### Scenario: No remaining Pino imports
   121→```
   122→Given Marcus has completed the Pino removal
   123→When he searches the codebase for pino imports
   124→Then zero files import from "pino" or reference the Pino logger instance
   125→And the pino package is not listed in package.json dependencies
   126→```
   127→
   128→#### Scenario: Request events captured as span events
   129→```
   130→Given the extraction pipeline processes a message from workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   131→When the extraction completes with 5 entities in 2341ms
   132→Then the extraction span contains an event "extraction.generate.completed"
   133→And the span attributes include entityCount: 5
   134→And the span duration reflects the 2341ms processing time
   135→```
   136→
   137→### Acceptance Criteria
   138→- [ ] `pino` removed from `package.json` dependencies
   139→- [ ] Zero files import from "pino" or reference Pino logger
   140→- [ ] `logInfo`, `logWarn`, `logError`, `logDebug` functions removed
   141→- [ ] `app/src/server/logging.ts` removed or replaced with thin console logger
   142→- [ ] Startup messages still visible in console during `bun run dev`
   143→- [ ] All former `logInfo`/`logError` call sites migrated to span events or span attributes
   144→
   145→### Technical Notes
   146→- Migration is mechanical: each `logInfo(event, data)` becomes span event or attribute
   147→- `elapsedMs()` utility may be retained if useful outside span contexts
   148→- `userFacingError()` in observability.ts is unrelated to logging -- keep it
   149→
   150→### Dependencies
   151→- US-OT01 (OTEL SDK Bootstrap) -- must have TracerProvider before migrating log calls to spans
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## US-OT03: AI SDK Telemetry on All LLM Calls
   156→
   157→### Problem
   158→Marcus has 19+ LLM call sites across extraction, chat, observer, PM agent, behavior scorer, onboarding, intent authorization, and analytics. When an LLM call produces wrong results, he has no visibility into model ID, token usage, latency, or function context without adding temporary logging and redeploying. He also cannot attribute token costs to specific functions.
   159→
   160→### Who
   161→- Developer/Operator | Debugging LLM behavior and managing costs | Needs per-call visibility without code changes
   162→
   163→### Solution
   164→Enable `experimental_telemetry` on every `generateObject`, `streamText`, and `ToolLoopAgent` call with a consistent `functionId` from the defined taxonomy and contextual metadata (workspaceId, messageId where available).
   165→
   166→### Jobs Served
   167→- Job 1 (Debug LLM Calls) -- primary
   168→- Job 4 (Cost Visibility) -- primary
   169→
   170→### Domain Examples
   171→#### 1: Extraction call with telemetry
   172→The extraction pipeline's `generateObject()` call in `app/src/server/extraction/` gets `experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true, functionId: 'brain.extraction.generate', metadata: { workspaceId: 'ws-marcus-dev', messageId: 'msg-a1b2c3' } }`. The resulting span shows model `anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku`, 1847 prompt tokens, 423 completion tokens, 2341ms duration.
   173→
   174→#### 2: Chat agent streaming with telemetry
   175→The `streamText()` call in `app/src/server/chat/handler.ts` gets `experimental_telemetry: { isEnabled: true, functionId: 'brain.chat.stream', metadata: { workspaceId: 'ws-marcus-dev', conversationId: 'conv-x7y8z9' } }`. The span captures the full streaming duration, total tokens, and model ID.
   176→
   177→#### 3: PM subagent with telemetry
   178→The PM agent's `generateObject()` in `app/src/server/agents/pm/agent.ts` gets `functionId: 'brain.pm.agent'`. When Marcus sees high token costs, he can filter spans by `brain.pm.agent` to see exactly how many tokens the PM agent consumes per invocation.
   179→
   180→### UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   181→
   182→#### Scenario: Extraction span emits token usage
   183→```
   184→Given a user sends a message containing "We decided to use SurrealDB for the graph layer" to workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   185→When the extraction pipeline runs generateObject with experimental_telemetry enabled
   186→Then a span is emitted with ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.extraction.generate"
   187→And the span includes ai.usage.promptTokens > 0
   188→And the span includes ai.usage.completionTokens > 0
   189→And the span includes ai.model.id matching the configured extraction model
   190→```
   191→
   192→#### Scenario: All LLM call sites have telemetry enabled
   193→```
   194→Given Marcus reviews all generateObject and streamText calls in the codebase
   195→When he checks each call for experimental_telemetry configuration
   196→Then every call includes isEnabled: true
   197→And every call includes a functionId from the defined taxonomy
   198→```
   199→
   200→#### Scenario: Function ID enables cost attribution
   201→```
   202→Given the system has processed 100 chat messages in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   203→When Marcus queries token usage spans grouped by ai.telemetry.functionId
   204→Then he can see separate token totals for extraction.generate, chat.stream, pm.agent, and other functions
   205→And the sum of per-function tokens matches the total token usage
   206→```
   207→
   208→#### Scenario: Telemetry metadata enables trace correlation
   209→```
   210→Given a chat message "msg-a1b2c3" triggers both extraction and chat agent LLM calls
   211→When Marcus searches spans by metadata messageId "msg-a1b2c3"
   212→Then he finds both the extraction span and the chat agent span
   213→And both spans are linked to the same parent HTTP trace
   214→```
   215→
   216→#### Scenario: Telemetry coexists with AI SDK devtools middleware
   217→```
   218→Given the Brain server uses @ai-sdk/devtools middleware on all model configurations
   219→When experimental_telemetry is also enabled on a generateObject call
   220→Then both devtools middleware and telemetry spans function without conflict
   221→And the span data is complete (model, tokens, duration)
   222→```
   223→
   224→### Acceptance Criteria
   225→- [ ] All `generateObject` calls (15 sites) include `experimental_telemetry` with `isEnabled: true` and valid `functionId`
   226→- [ ] All `streamText` calls (2 sites) include `experimental_telemetry` with `isEnabled: true` and valid `functionId`
   227→- [ ] `ToolLoopAgent` calls include telemetry configuration
   228→- [ ] Function IDs follow the `brain.{domain}.{operation}` taxonomy
   229→- [ ] Telemetry metadata includes `workspaceId` where available
   230→- [ ] Telemetry does not conflict with `@ai-sdk/devtools` middleware
   231→
   232→### Technical Notes
   233→- Consider a factory function `createTelemetryConfig(functionId, metadata)` to reduce boilerplate
   234→- `@ai-sdk/devtools` middleware wraps models in `dependencies.ts` -- telemetry is per-call, not per-model, so they should coexist
   235→- Token metadata fields come from AI SDK spans automatically: `ai.usage.promptTokens`, `ai.usage.completionTokens`
   236→
   237→### Dependencies
   238→- US-OT01 (OTEL SDK Bootstrap) -- TracerProvider must be active for AI SDK to emit spans
   239→
   240→---
   241→
   242→## US-OT04: HTTP Request Tracing
   243→
   244→### Problem
   245→Marcus currently has `withRequestLogging()` which logs method, route, status, and duration as flat Pino events. When a request is slow, he sees the total duration but cannot see which internal operation (extraction? LLM call? DB query?) consumed the time. He must mentally correlate timestamps across separate log lines.
   246→
   247→### Who
   248→- Developer/Operator | Diagnosing slow or failed requests | Needs a connected trace showing the full request waterfall
   249→
   250→### Solution
   251→Replace `withRequestLogging()` with an OTEL-based request wrapper that creates a root span for each HTTP request. Internal operations (extraction, chat processing, tool calls) create child spans that automatically nest under the root. The result is a trace waterfall showing the complete request lifecycle.
   252→
   253→### Jobs Served
   254→- Job 2 (Request Tracing) -- primary
   255→- Job 3 (Operational Monitoring) -- supports HTTP metrics
   256→
   257→### Domain Examples
   258→#### 1: Chat message request trace
   259→Marcus sends a chat message. The root span `brain.http.request POST /api/chat/messages` shows 8234ms total. Child spans show: `brain.chat.ingress` (45ms), `brain.chat.process` (8180ms) containing `brain.extraction.pipeline` (2100ms) and `brain.chat.agent` (5200ms). The chat agent span further contains `ai.streamText` (4100ms) and `brain.chat.tool.invoke_pm_agent` (2800ms). Bottleneck: chat agent LLM call at 50% of total.
   260→
   261→#### 2: Entity search request trace
   262→Marcus searches for entities. Root span `brain.http.request GET /api/entities/search` shows 120ms. Child span `brain.entity.fulltext-search` shows 85ms. No LLM calls involved. Fast path confirmed.
   263→
   264→#### 3: Failed request trace
   265→A chat message fails because SurrealDB is unreachable. Root span `brain.http.request POST /api/chat/messages` shows status ERROR. Child span `brain.chat.persist-message` has `span.recordException(ConnectionUnavailableError)`. Marcus sees exactly which operation failed without reading through log lines.
   266→
   267→### UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   268→
   269→#### Scenario: Root span created for every HTTP request
   270→```
   271→Given Marcus sends a POST request to /api/chat/messages in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   272→When the request completes
   273→Then a root span "brain.http.request" exists with attributes method: "POST", route: "/api/chat/messages"
   274→And the span duration reflects the total request processing time
   275→And the span status reflects the HTTP response status
   276→```
   277→
   278→#### Scenario: Child spans nest under root span
   279→```
   280→Given Marcus sends a chat message that triggers extraction and chat agent processing
   281→When the request completes
   282→Then the extraction pipeline span is a child of the root HTTP span
   283→And the chat agent span is a child of the root HTTP span
   284→And the AI SDK LLM spans are children of their respective pipeline spans
   285→```
   286→
   287→#### Scenario: Request ID preserved as span attribute
   288→```
   289→Given Marcus sends a request that generates requestId "req-d4e5f6"
   290→When the request trace is recorded
   291→Then the root span includes attribute requestId: "req-d4e5f6"
   292→And the requestId is available for backward-compatible log correlation
   293→```
   294→
   295→#### Scenario: Failed request records exception
   296→```
   297→Given the SurrealDB connection is unavailable
   298→When Marcus sends a POST request to /api/chat/messages
   299→Then the root span status is ERROR
   300→And the span contains a recorded exception with the ConnectionUnavailableError details
   301→And the span duration reflects the time until failure
   302→```
   303→
   304→### Acceptance Criteria
   305→- [ ] Every HTTP route handler creates a root span with method, route, and status attributes
   306→- [ ] Pipeline stages (extraction, chat processing, tool calls) create child spans
   307→- [ ] AI SDK telemetry spans automatically nest under the active OTEL context
   308→- [ ] `requestId` is carried as a span attribute on the root span
   309→- [ ] Failed requests record exceptions on the span with ERROR status
   310→- [ ] `withRequestLogging()` is removed (replaced by span-based tracing)
   311→
   312→### Technical Notes
   313→- Must propagate OTEL context through `AsyncLocalStorage` boundaries
   314→- SSE streaming: root span may end when headers are sent; streaming content spans are separate
   315→- `request-context.ts` coexistence -- do not remove `AsyncLocalStorage`, layer OTEL context alongside it
   316→- Consider whether `elapsedMs()` is still needed or if span timing replaces it entirely
   317→
   318→### Dependencies
   319→- US-OT01 (OTEL SDK Bootstrap) -- TracerProvider must be active
   320→- US-OT02 (Pino Removal) -- `withRequestLogging` is removed in this story
   321→
   322→---
   323→
   324→## US-OT05: Operational Metrics
   325→
   326→### Problem
   327→Marcus has no quantitative health metrics for the Brain platform. He cannot answer "what is the p95 latency of chat responses?" or "how many extraction errors occurred this week?" without manually analyzing log files. There is no alerting -- degradation is discovered when users report issues.
   328→
   329→### Who
   330→- Developer/Operator | Monitoring production health | Needs continuous quantitative metrics with alerting capability
   331→
   332→### Solution
   333→Define OTEL metric instruments (histograms and counters) for LLM call duration, token usage, error counts, HTTP request duration, and request volume. Record metrics at instrumentation points. Export via console in dev and OTLP in prod.
   334→
   335→### Jobs Served
   336→- Job 3 (Operational Monitoring) -- primary
   337→- Job 4 (Cost Visibility) -- supports token counters
   338→
   339→### Domain Examples
   340→#### 1: LLM latency histogram
   341→After processing 500 chat messages, Marcus queries the `brain.llm.duration` histogram filtered by `functionId=brain.chat.stream`. He sees p50=3200ms, p95=5800ms, p99=8400ms. The p95 is above his 5000ms target -- he investigates prompt length.
   342→
   343→#### 2: Token counter for cost projection
   344→Marcus queries `brain.llm.tokens.prompt` and `brain.llm.tokens.completion` counters grouped by `functionId` over the past 7 days. Extraction consumed 1.2M prompt tokens and 340K completion tokens. Chat consumed 890K/520K. He calculates weekly cost at $14.95 and projects monthly at $64.
   345→
   346→#### 3: HTTP error rate detection
   347→The `brain.http.requests` counter with `status_code=500` shows 15 errors in the past hour, up from a baseline of 0-2. The `brain.http.duration` histogram for the affected route shows p50 jumped from 200ms to 8000ms. SurrealDB connection issue confirmed.
   348→
   349→### UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   350→
   351→#### Scenario: LLM duration histogram records call latency
   352→```
   353→Given the extraction pipeline processes a message with a generateObject call taking 2341ms
   354→When the call completes
   355→Then the brain.llm.duration histogram records a value of 2341
   356→And the recording includes attributes functionId: "brain.extraction.generate" and model matching the configured extraction model
   357→```
   358→
   359→#### Scenario: Token counters increment on LLM call
   360→```
   361→Given a generateObject call uses 1847 prompt tokens and 423 completion tokens
   362→When the call completes
   363→Then brain.llm.tokens.prompt counter increments by 1847 with functionId attribute
   364→And brain.llm.tokens.completion counter increments by 423 with functionId attribute
   365→```
   366→
   367→#### Scenario: HTTP request metrics recorded
   368→```
   369→Given Marcus sends a GET request to /api/entities/search that returns 200 in 120ms
   370→When the request completes
   371→Then brain.http.requests counter increments by 1 with attributes method: "GET", route: "/api/entities/search", status_code: "200"
   372→And brain.http.duration histogram records 120 with the same attributes
   373→```
   374→
   375→#### Scenario: Error counter increments on LLM failure
   376→```
   377→Given a generateObject call to the observer verification fails with a model timeout
   378→When the error is caught
   379→Then brain.llm.errors counter increments by 1
   380→And the recording includes attributes functionId: "brain.observer.verify" and error_type: "timeout"
   381→```
   382→
   383→#### Scenario: Metrics export via OTLP
   384→```
   385→Given Marcus has set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to "http://collector:4318"
   386→When metrics have been recorded over a 60-second interval
   387→Then metric data is exported to the OTLP endpoint
   388→And the export includes all defined metric instruments
   389→```
   390→
   391→### Acceptance Criteria
   392→- [ ] `brain.llm.duration` histogram records latency for every LLM call with functionId and model attributes
   393→- [ ] `brain.llm.tokens.prompt` and `brain.llm.tokens.completion` counters record token usage per LLM call
   394→- [ ] `brain.llm.errors` counter records LLM failures with error_type attribute
   395→- [ ] `brain.http.duration` histogram records request latency with method, route, status_code
   396→- [ ] `brain.http.requests` counter records request volume with method, route, status_code
   397→- [ ] `brain.extraction.entities` counter records entity volume by type
   398→- [ ] Metrics export to console in dev and OTLP when endpoint is configured
   399→
   400→### Technical Notes
   401→- Metric instruments should be created once at initialization, not per-request
   402→- Token usage available from AI SDK telemetry span attributes -- may need integration hook to also record as metrics
   403→- Consider OTEL SDK's `View` API for histogram bucket configuration
   404→- Export interval configurable via `OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL` (default 60s)
   405→
   406→### Dependencies
   407→- US-OT01 (OTEL SDK Bootstrap) -- MeterProvider must be active
   408→- US-OT03 (AI SDK Telemetry) -- token usage comes from AI SDK spans
   409→

1→# Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin): OpenTelemetry Observability Migration
     2→
     3→## Feature: OTEL SDK Bootstrap
     4→
     5→```gherkin
     6→Feature: OpenTelemetry SDK initialization and exporter configuration
     7→  As a developer/operator of the Brain platform
     8→  I need the OTEL SDK to initialize at startup with appropriate exporters
     9→  So that all downstream instrumentation has a functioning telemetry pipeline
    10→
    11→  Background:
    12→    Given the Brain server application is configured to start
    13→
    14→  Scenario: Console exporter active by default in development
    15→    Given no OTEL environment variables are set
    16→    When the Brain server starts and processes a request to POST /api/chat/messages
    17→    Then span data appears in the terminal console output
    18→    And the span includes resource attribute service.name "brain"
    19→    And the span includes the HTTP method and route
    20→
    21→  Scenario: OTLP exporter active when endpoint is configured
    22→    Given OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set to "http://collector:4318"
    23→    And OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is set to "brain-prod"
    24→    When the Brain server starts
    25→    Then the TracerProvider uses OTLPTraceExporter targeting "http://collector:4318"
    26→    And the MeterProvider uses OTLPMetricExporter targeting "http://collector:4318"
    27→    And no span data appears in the console
    28→
    29→  Scenario: Graceful degradation when OTEL SDK fails to initialize
    30→    Given the Bun runtime has an incompatibility with the OTEL async hooks API
    31→    When the Brain server starts
    32→    Then the server starts successfully and accepts HTTP requests
    33→    And a warning "OTEL SDK failed to initialize, telemetry disabled" appears in console
    34→    And all tracer.startSpan calls return no-op spans
    35→    And all meter.createHistogram calls return no-op instruments
    36→
    37→  Scenario: Both TracerProvider and MeterProvider initialize
    38→    Given default server configuration
    39→    When the Brain server starts
    40→    Then TracerProvider is active and can create spans
    41→    And MeterProvider is active and can create metric instruments
    42→    And both providers use the same service.name resource attribute
    43→
    44→  Scenario: OTEL initialization completes before route handlers register
    45→    Given the server startup sequence
    46→    When the application bootstrap runs
    47→    Then the OTEL SDK initialization completes before Bun.serve() is called
    48→    And the first HTTP request processed has an active TracerProvider
    49→```
    50→
    51→## Feature: Pino Removal and Console Logger
    52→
    53→```gherkin
    54→Feature: Remove Pino structured logging and replace with OTEL spans
    55→  As a developer maintaining the Brain codebase
    56→  I need Pino removed cleanly without losing startup visibility
    57→  So that the codebase has a single telemetry system
    58→
    59→  Scenario: Pino dependency removed from project
    60→    Given the migration is complete
    61→    When the project dependencies are inspected
    62→    Then "pino" does not appear in package.json dependencies or devDependencies
    63→    And no source file contains an import from "pino"
    64→    And no source file references the Pino logger instance
    65→
    66→  Scenario: Startup messages visible without Pino
    67→    Given Pino has been removed
    68→    When Marcus starts the Brain server with "bun run dev"
    69→    Then the server port number appears in console output
    70→    And the database connection status appears in console output
    71→    And migration status appears in console output
    72→    And no output uses Pino's JSON format
    73→
    74→  Scenario: Request-scoped events migrated to span events
    75→    Given the extraction pipeline processes a message in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
    76→    And the extraction takes 2341ms and produces 5 entities
    77→    When the extraction completes
    78→    Then the extraction span duration is approximately 2341ms
    79→    And the span includes attribute entityCount: 5
    80→    And no Pino logInfo call is made for this event
    81→
    82→  Scenario: Error events migrated to span exceptions
    83→    Given the observer verification LLM call fails with a timeout error
    84→    When the error is caught
    85→    Then the observer span records the exception with the timeout error details
    86→    And the span status is set to ERROR
    87→    And no Pino logError call is made for this event
    88→
    89→  Scenario: logInfo, logWarn, logError, logDebug functions removed
    90→    Given the migration is complete
    91→    When the codebase is searched for logInfo, logWarn, logError, or logDebug function definitions
    92→    Then zero definitions are found
    93→    And zero call sites reference these functions
    94→```
    95→
    96→## Feature: AI SDK Telemetry on All LLM Calls
    97→
    98→```gherkin
    99→Feature: Enable experimental_telemetry on every AI SDK call
   100→  As an operator debugging LLM behavior and tracking costs
   101→  I need every LLM call to emit structured telemetry spans
   102→  So that I can inspect any call's model, tokens, latency, and function context
   103→
   104→  Background:
   105→    Given the OTEL SDK is initialized with TracerProvider active
   106→
   107→  Scenario: Extraction generateObject emits telemetry span
   108→    Given a user sends message "We need to migrate the billing API to tRPC" to workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   109→    When the extraction pipeline calls generateObject with experimental_telemetry enabled
   110→    Then a span is emitted with attribute ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.extraction.generate"
   111→    And the span includes ai.model.id matching the configured EXTRACTION_MODEL
   112→    And the span includes ai.usage.promptTokens as a positive integer
   113→    And the span includes ai.usage.completionTokens as a positive integer
   114→    And the span includes telemetry metadata workspaceId "ws-marcus-dev"
   115→
   116→  Scenario: Chat agent streamText emits telemetry span
   117→    Given Marcus has an active conversation in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   118→    When the chat agent calls streamText with experimental_telemetry enabled
   119→    Then a span is emitted with attribute ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.chat.stream"
   120→    And the span duration covers the full streaming time
   121→    And the span includes total token usage after streaming completes
   122→
   123→  Scenario: PM subagent generateObject emits telemetry span
   124→    Given the chat agent invokes the PM subagent with intent "plan_work"
   125→    When the PM agent calls generateObject with experimental_telemetry enabled
   126→    Then a span is emitted with attribute ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.pm.agent"
   127→    And the span is a child of the chat agent's tool invocation span
   128→
   129→  Scenario: Observer verification emits telemetry span
   130→    Given the observer scans the graph and finds a potential contradiction
   131→    When it calls generateObject to verify the observation
   132→    Then a span is emitted with attribute ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.observer.verify"
   133→    And the span includes the model ID and token counts
   134→
   135→  Scenario: Behavior scorer emits telemetry span
   136→    Given a behavior scoring request is made for workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   137→    When the scorer calls generateObject with experimental_telemetry enabled
   138→    Then a span is emitted with attribute ai.telemetry.functionId "brain.behavior.score"
   139→
   140→  Scenario: All call sites verified for telemetry coverage
   141→    Given a developer audits all generateObject and streamText calls in the codebase
   142→    When each call is inspected
   143→    Then every call includes experimental_telemetry with isEnabled: true
   144→    And every call has a functionId from the approved taxonomy starting with "brain."
   145→    And no call uses a functionId outside the defined taxonomy
   146→
   147→  Scenario: Telemetry coexists with AI SDK devtools middleware
   148→    Given the extraction model is wrapped with @ai-sdk/devtools middleware
   149→    And experimental_telemetry is enabled on the generateObject call
   150→    When the call executes
   151→    Then both devtools tracing and OTEL telemetry spans are emitted
   152→    And the OTEL span includes complete token usage and model information
   153→
   154→  Scenario: Telemetry metadata enables cross-call correlation
   155→    Given message "msg-r8s9t0" triggers both extraction and chat agent LLM calls
   156→    When both calls complete
   157→    Then both spans include telemetry metadata messageId "msg-r8s9t0"
   158→    And a trace query filtering by messageId returns both spans
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Feature: HTTP Request Distributed Tracing
   162→
   163→```gherkin
   164→Feature: Distributed tracing for HTTP request lifecycle
   165→  As an operator diagnosing slow or failed requests
   166→  I need a connected trace showing the full request waterfall
   167→  So that I can pinpoint where time is spent without correlating log timestamps
   168→
   169→  Background:
   170→    Given the OTEL SDK is initialized with TracerProvider active
   171→
   172→  Scenario: Root span created for chat message request
   173→    Given Marcus sends a POST request to /api/chat/messages in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
   174→    When the request completes with status 200
   175→    Then a root span "brain.http.request" exists
   176→    And the span has attribute http.method: "POST"
   177→    And the span has attribute http.route: "/api/chat/messages"
   178→    And the span has attribute http.status_code: 200
   179→    And the span duration reflects the total request time
   180→
   181→  Scenario: Pipeline child spans nest under root
   182→    Given Marcus sends a chat message that triggers extraction and chat agent
   183→    When the request completes
   184→    Then span "brain.extraction.pipeline" is a child of the root HTTP span
   185→    And span "brain.chat.agent" is a child of the root HTTP span
   186→    And the AI SDK span for extraction is a child of "brain.extraction.pipeline"
   187→    And all child span start/end times fall within the root span duration
   188→
   189→  Scenario: Trace waterfall reveals bottleneck
   190→    Given Marcus sends a chat message where the chat agent LLM call takes 4100ms
   191→    And the total request takes 8234ms
   192→    When Marcus views the trace waterfall
   193→    Then the chat agent LLM span shows 4100ms (approximately 50% of total)
   194→    And Marcus can identify the LLM call as the bottleneck without timestamp correlation
   195→
   196→  Scenario: Request ID preserved for backward compatibility
   197→    Given the request context assigns requestId "req-u1v2w3"
   198→    When the root HTTP span is created
   199→    Then the span includes attribute requestId: "req-u1v2w3"
   200→
   201→  Scenario: Failed request traced with exception
   202→    Given the SurrealDB connection is unavailable
   203→    When Marcus sends a POST request to /api/chat/messages
   204→    Then the root span status is ERROR
   205→    And the failing child span records a ConnectionUnavailableError exception
   206→    And the trace shows exactly which operation failed and its parent chain
   207→
   208→  Scenario: Entity search request traced
   209→    Given Marcus sends a GET request to /api/entities/search?q=authentication
   210→    When the request completes with status 200 in 120ms
   211→    Then a root span "brain.http.request" exists with method GET and route "/api/entities/search"
   212→    And the span duration is approximately 120ms
   213→
   214→  Scenario: Context propagation across async boundaries
   215→    Given a chat message handler spawns async work tracked via inflight tracker
   216→    When the async extraction pipeline runs in a different async context
   217→    Then the extraction span still correctly parents under the root HTTP span
   218→    And the OTEL context propagates through AsyncLocalStorage boundaries
   219→```
   220→
   221→## Feature: Operational Metrics
   222→
   223→```gherkin
   224→Feature: OTEL metrics for health monitoring and cost attribution
   225→  As an operator monitoring production health
   226→  I need quantitative metrics on LLM calls, HTTP requests, and entity extraction
   227→  So that I can detect degradation and attribute costs to specific functions
   228→
   229→  Background:
   230→    Given the OTEL SDK is initialized with MeterProvider active
   231→
   232→  Scenario: LLM call latency recorded as histogram
   233→    Given the extraction pipeline calls generateObject and the call takes 2341ms
   234→    When the call completes successfully
   235→    Then the brain.llm.duration histogram records value 2341
   236→    And the recording has attribute functionId: "brain.extraction.generate"
   237→    And the recording has attribute model matching the configured EXTRACTION_MODEL
   238→
   239→  Scenario: Prompt tokens recorded as counter
   240→    Given a generateObject call uses 1847 prompt tokens
   241→    When the call completes
   242→    Then brain.llm.tokens.prompt increments by 1847
   243→    And the increment has attribute functionId matching the call's function ID
   244→
   245→  Scenario: Completion tokens recorded as counter
   246→    Given a generateObject call produces 423 completion tokens
   247→    When the call completes
   248→    Then brain.llm.tokens.completion increments by 423
   249→    And the increment has attribute functionId matching the call's function ID
   250→
   251→  Scenario: LLM error recorded as counter
   252→    Given a generateObject call to brain.observer.verify fails with error type "timeout"
   253→    When the error is caught
   254→    Then brain.llm.errors increments by 1
   255→    And the increment has attributes functionId: "brain.observer.verify" and error_type: "timeout"
   256→
   257→  Scenario: HTTP request duration recorded as histogram
   258→    Given a GET request to /api/entities/search completes in 120ms with status 200
   259→    When the response is sent
   260→    Then brain.http.duration histogram records value 120
   261→    And the recording has attributes method: "GET", route: "/api/entities/search", status_code: "200"
   262→
   263→  Scenario: HTTP request count recorded as counter
   264→    Given 50 requests have been processed in the last minute
   265→    When the metric export interval fires
   266→    Then brain.http.requests counter reflects 50 total increments
   267→    And each increment has method, route, and status_code attributes
   268→
   269→  Scenario: Entity extraction count recorded
   270→    Given the extraction pipeline produces 3 task entities and 2 decision entities
   271→    When the entities are persisted
   272→    Then brain.extraction.entities increments by 3 with attribute entity_type: "task"
   273→    And brain.extraction.entities increments by 2 with attribute entity_type: "decision"
   274→
   275→  Scenario: Token counters enable cost attribution by function
   276→    Given the system has processed 100 messages over 7 days
   277→    When Marcus queries brain.llm.tokens.prompt grouped by functionId
   278→    Then he sees separate totals for each function (extraction.generate, chat.stream, pm.agent, etc.)
   279→    And the per-function totals sum to the overall total
   280→
   281→  @property
   282→  Scenario: Metric recording does not block request processing
   283→    Given the system is handling concurrent requests
   284→    Then metric recording operations complete in under 1ms
   285→    And no request is delayed by metric export operations
   286→    And metric export runs asynchronously on a separate interval
   287→
   288→  @property
   289→  Scenario: Metrics survive OTLP endpoint outage
   290→    Given the OTLP endpoint becomes unreachable
   291→    Then the server continues processing requests without errors
   292→    And metrics are buffered in memory
   293→    And metric export resumes when the endpoint is reachable again
   294→```
   295→

1→# JTBD Job Stories: OpenTelemetry Observability Migration
     2→
     3→## Job 1: Debug LLM Calls
     4→
     5→### Job Story
     6→
     7→**When** an extraction or chat response produces unexpected results (wrong entities, hallucinated relationships, missing fields),
     8→**I want to** see the full LLM request/response including prompt content, model parameters, latency, and token usage,
     9→**so I can** determine whether the root cause is a prompt issue, model regression, or malformed input data -- without adding ad-hoc logging and redeploying.
    10→
    11→### Functional Job
    12→Inspect the complete lifecycle of any LLM call: input prompt, model configuration, raw response, token counts, and timing -- after the fact, without code changes.
    13→
    14→### Emotional Job
    15→Feel in control when diagnosing LLM misbehavior. Replace the anxiety of "something is wrong and I cannot see why" with confidence that the full picture is visible.
    16→
    17→### Social Job
    18→Be seen as someone who debugs LLM issues methodically with evidence, not by guessing and redeploying blindly.
    19→
    20→### Outcome Statements
    21→- Minimize the time it takes to identify the root cause of a wrong LLM response
    22→- Minimize the likelihood of misattributing an LLM failure (prompt vs model vs data)
    23→- Maximize the completeness of diagnostic information available without code changes
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Job 2: Request Tracing
    28→
    29→### Job Story
    30→
    31→**When** a user reports that a chat interaction was slow or returned an error,
    32→**I want to** trace the full request lifecycle from HTTP ingress through chat agent orchestration, tool calls, LLM invocations, and database queries,
    33→**so I can** pinpoint exactly where time was spent or where the failure occurred -- without correlating timestamps across separate log files.
    34→
    35→### Functional Job
    36→Follow a single user request through every system boundary (HTTP handler, extraction pipeline, chat agent, PM subagent, tool execution, SurrealDB queries) with timing and status at each hop.
    37→
    38→### Emotional Job
    39→Feel confident that slow or broken requests can be diagnosed quickly. Replace the dread of "I have to grep through logs and mentally reconstruct the sequence" with a clear, connected trace.
    40→
    41→### Social Job
    42→Demonstrate to the team that production issues are diagnosable in minutes, not hours -- building trust in the system's operational maturity.
    43→
    44→### Outcome Statements
    45→- Minimize the time it takes to pinpoint the slowest operation in a request chain
    46→- Minimize the number of tools/files needed to reconstruct a request's lifecycle
    47→- Maximize the likelihood of identifying the root cause on the first investigation attempt
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Job 3: Operational Monitoring
    52→
    53→### Job Story
    54→
    55→**When** the Brain system is running in production serving real users,
    56→**I want to** see dashboards with LLM latency distributions, token cost trends, error rates by endpoint, and throughput over time,
    57→**so I can** detect degradation before users notice and make capacity decisions based on data rather than guesswork.
    58→
    59→### Functional Job
    60→Collect and expose quantitative metrics: LLM call latency (p50/p95/p99), token usage per model, HTTP error rates, request throughput, and extraction pipeline duration -- continuously, with minimal performance overhead.
    61→
    62→### Emotional Job
    63→Feel assured that the system is healthy without having to actively check. Replace the background worry of "is it working?" with the calm of instrumented visibility.
    64→
    65→### Social Job
    66→Present production health data to stakeholders with concrete numbers, demonstrating operational rigor and justifying infrastructure investments.
    67→
    68→### Outcome Statements
    69→- Minimize the time between a performance degradation starting and being detected
    70→- Minimize the likelihood of a user-visible outage that could have been predicted
    71→- Maximize the accuracy of cost projections for LLM usage
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Job 4: Cost Visibility
    76→
    77→### Job Story
    78→
    79→**When** I am evaluating whether to switch models, optimize prompts, or adjust extraction thresholds,
    80→**I want to** see token usage breakdowns by function (extraction vs chat agent vs observer vs PM agent vs behavior scorer),
    81→**so I can** identify which functions consume the most tokens and make informed decisions about where optimization effort will have the highest ROI.
    82→
    83→### Functional Job
    84→Attribute token consumption (prompt tokens, completion tokens) to specific application functions and models, aggregated over configurable time windows.
    85→
    86→### Emotional Job
    87→Feel empowered to make cost decisions with real data. Replace the uncertainty of "I think extraction is expensive but I am not sure" with precise per-function cost breakdowns.
    88→
    89→### Social Job
    90→Justify model and prompt optimization decisions to the team with concrete usage data, not intuition.
    91→
    92→### Outcome Statements
    93→- Minimize the time it takes to identify the highest-cost LLM function
    94→- Minimize the likelihood of optimizing a function that is not actually the cost bottleneck
    95→- Maximize the precision of per-function token cost attribution
    96→

input.new_string:
### What Gets Removed
1. `pino` dependency (v10.3.1)
2. `app/src/server/logging.ts` -- Pino configuration
3. `logInfo`, `logWarn`, `logError`, `logDebug` from `app/src/server/http/observability.ts`
4. `withRequestLogging` from `app/src/server/http/request-logging.ts`
5. All `logInfo("event.name", { data })` call sites across the codebase

### What Gets Added
1. `app/src/server/telemetry/init.ts` -- OTEL provider initialization (TracerProvider, MeterProvider, LoggerProvider)
2. `app/src/server/telemetry/ai-telemetry.ts` -- AI SDK telemetry config factory
3. `app/src/server/telemetry/metrics.ts` -- metric instrument definitions
4. `app/src/server/telemetry/function-ids.ts` -- function ID constants
5. `app/src/server/telemetry/logger.ts` -- thin wrapper over OTEL Logs API for ergonomic `log.info()`/`log.warn()`/`log.error()`/`log.debug()` usage
6. HTTP request tracing wrapper (replaces `withRequestLogging`)

### Log Migration Strategy
All existing `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` call sites migrate to the OTEL logger wrapper:
- `logInfo("event.name", { key: value })` becomes `log.info("event.name", { key: value })` using OTEL Logs API under the hood
- The wrapper calls `logger.emit({ body, severityText, attributes })` on the OTEL logger obtained via `logs.getLogger('brain-server')`
- Logs emitted within an active span automatically inherit `trace_id` and `span_id` from the OTEL context -- no manual correlation needed
- LoggerProvider configured alongside TracerProvider and MeterProvider: console log exporter in dev, OTLP log exporter in prod
- Startup/config logs emitted before OTEL SDK initialization gracefully degrade to `console.log` output

input.old_string:
### Solution
Remove Pino dependency and all `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` call sites. Replace with a thin console-based logger for startup messages only. All request-scoped logging moves to OTEL spans.
input.new_string:
### Solution
Remove Pino dependency and migrate all `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` call sites to the OTEL Logs API. A thin wrapper provides ergonomic `log.info()`/`log.warn()`/`log.error()`/`log.debug()` functions that call `logger.emit()` on the OTEL logger (`logs.getLogger('brain-server')`). Logs emitted within an active span automatically inherit `trace_id` and `span_id` for trace correlation. LoggerProvider is configured alongside TracerProvider and MeterProvider: console log exporter in dev, OTLP log exporter in prod. Startup messages emitted before OTEL initialization gracefully fall back to `console.log`.

input.old_string:
### Domain Examples
#### 1: Startup message migration
`logInfo("server.started", { port: 3000 })` becomes `console.log("[brain] Server started on port 3000")`. Simple, no structured JSON needed for startup.

#### 2: Request-scoped event migration
`logInfo("extraction.generate.completed", { durationMs: 2341, entityCount: 5 })` becomes a span event on the extraction span: `span.addEvent("extraction.generate.completed", { entityCount: 5 })` with duration captured by the span itself.

#### 3: Error logging migration
`logError("observer.llm.error", error, { functionId: "verify" })` becomes `span.recordException(error)` with `span.setStatus({ code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR })`.
input.new_string:
### Domain Examples
#### 1: Startup message migration (pre-init graceful degradation)
`logInfo("server.started", { port: 3000 })` becomes `log.info("server.started", { port: 3000 })`. If OTEL SDK has not yet initialized, the wrapper falls back to `console.log("[brain] server.started { port: 3000 }")`. After initialization, the same call emits a structured OTEL log record.

#### 2: Request-scoped log with automatic trace correlation
`logInfo("extraction.generate.completed", { durationMs: 2341, entityCount: 5 })` becomes `log.info("extraction.generate.completed", { durationMs: 2341, entityCount: 5 })`. Because this runs within an active extraction span, the emitted OTEL log record automatically includes the `trace_id` and `span_id` from the current context. In a trace backend, Marcus can see this log alongside its parent span.

#### 3: Error logging migration
`logError("observer.llm.error", error, { functionId: "verify" })` becomes `log.error("observer.llm.error", { error: error.message, functionId: "verify" })`. The OTEL log record has `severityText: "ERROR"` and carries the active `trace_id`/`span_id`. Span-level exception recording (`span.recordException(error)`) is used in addition for trace-aware error visibility.

#### 4: Debug-level logging
`logDebug("chat.tool.resolved", { toolName: "search_entities", resultCount: 12 })` becomes `log.debug("chat.tool.resolved", { toolName: "search_entities", resultCount: 12 })`. The OTEL log record has `severityText: "DEBUG"`. Debug logs can be filtered at the exporter/collector level without code changes.

input.new_string:
### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pino` removed from `package.json` dependencies
- [ ] Zero files import from "pino" or reference Pino logger
- [ ] `logInfo`, `logWarn`, `logError`, `logDebug` Pino-based functions removed from `observability.ts`
- [ ] `app/src/server/logging.ts` removed (Pino configuration)
- [ ] Thin OTEL logger wrapper created (e.g. `app/src/server/telemetry/logger.ts`) providing `log.info()`/`log.warn()`/`log.error()`/`log.debug()`
- [ ] All former `logInfo`/`logWarn`/`logError`/`logDebug` call sites migrated to OTEL logger wrapper
- [ ] Log records emitted within a traced context include `trace_id` and `span_id`
- [ ] Log severity levels correctly map: debug/info/warn/error
- [ ] Logs export via console in dev and OTLP when endpoint is configured
- [ ] Startup messages emitted before OTEL initialization fall back to console without crashing

### Technical Notes
- Migration is mechanical: each `logInfo(event, data)` becomes `log.info(event, data)` using the OTEL wrapper
- The wrapper uses `@opentelemetry/api-logs`: `logs.getLogger('brain-server').emit({ body, severityText, attributes })`
- Logs within an active span automatically inherit trace context -- no manual `trace_id` injection needed
- The wrapper should check if LoggerProvider is initialized; if not, fall back to `console` methods
- `elapsedMs()` utility may be retained if useful outside span contexts
- `userFacingError()` in observability.ts is unrelated to logging -- keep it
- Some call sites may also add span events (`span.addEvent()`) in addition to OTEL logs where trace-level detail is valuable

```gherkin
Feature: Remove Pino structured logging and replace with OTEL Logs API
  As a developer maintaining the Brain codebase
  I need Pino removed and all logging migrated to the OTEL Logs API
  So that the codebase has a single telemetry system with trace-correlated logs

  Scenario: Pino dependency removed from project
    Given the migration is complete
    When the project dependencies are inspected
    Then "pino" does not appear in package.json dependencies or devDependencies
    And no source file contains an import from "pino"

    Then the server port number appears in console output
    And the database connection status appears in console output
    And migration status appears in console output
    And no output uses Pino's JSON format

  Scenario: logInfo, logWarn, logError, logDebug Pino wrappers removed
    Given the migration is complete
    When the codebase is searched for logInfo, logWarn, logError, or logDebug function definitions
    Then zero Pino-based definitions are found
    And all former call sites have been migrated to the OTEL logger wrapper

  Scenario: Request-scoped events migrated to OTEL log records
    Given the extraction pipeline processes a message in workspace "ws-marcus-dev"
    And the extraction takes 2341ms and produces 5 entities
    When log.info("extraction.generate.completed", { entityCount: 5 }) is called
    Then an OTEL log record is emitted with body "extraction.generate.completed"
    And the log record includes attribute entityCount: 5
    And no Pino logInfo call is made for this event

  Scenario: Error events migrated to OTEL error logs
    Given the observer verification LLM call fails with a timeout error
    When the error is caught
    Then log.error() emits an OTEL log record with severityText "ERROR"
    And the observer span records the exception with the timeout error details
    And the span status is set to ERROR
    And no Pino logError call is made for this event

  Scenario: Log output includes trace_id and span_id within traced context
    Given the OTEL SDK is initialized with TracerProvider and LoggerProvider active
    And an HTTP request to POST /api/chat/messages creates a root span
    When log.info("chat.processing.started", { conversationId: "conv-x7y8z9" }) is called within the request handler
    Then the emitted OTEL log record includes trace_id matching the root span's trace
    And the log record includes span_id matching the active span
    And the trace_id and span_id are visible in the exported log output

  Scenario: Log severity levels map correctly to OTEL severity
    Given the OTEL LoggerProvider is active
    When log.debug("debug message") is called
    Then the log record has severityText "DEBUG" and severityNumber 5
    When log.info("info message") is called
    Then the log record has severityText "INFO" and severityNumber 9
    When log.warn("warn message") is called
    Then the log record has severityText "WARN" and severityNumber 13
    When log.error("error message") is called
    Then the log record has severityText "ERROR" and severityNumber 17

  Scenario: Logs exported via same OTEL exporter pipeline as traces
    Given Marcus has set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to "http://collector:4318"
    When application logs are emitted during request processing
    Then log records are exported to the OTLP endpoint via OTLPLogExporter
    And no log output appears in the console
    When no OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set
    Then log records appear in the console via ConsoleLogRecordExporter

  Scenario: Startup logs work before OTEL SDK is fully initialized
    Given the server bootstrap sequence has not yet called OTEL SDK init
    When log.info("config.loaded", { port: 3000, db: "surreal" }) is called during early config
    Then the message appears in console output via graceful fallback
    And no error or crash occurs due to uninitialized LoggerProvider
    And after OTEL SDK initializes, subsequent log.info() calls emit OTEL log records

  Scenario: Logs without active span omit trace context gracefully
    Given the OTEL LoggerProvider is active
    And no span is currently active (e.g. a background timer callback)

    And the log record does not include trace_id or span_id

```

input.old_string:
- Minimize the likelihood of optimizing a function that is not actually the cost bottleneck
- Maximize the precision of per-function token cost attribution
input.new_string:
- Minimize the likelihood of optimizing a function that is not actually the cost bottleneck
- Maximize the precision of per-function token cost attribution

---

## Job 5: Log-Trace Correlation

### Job Story

**When** I am investigating a specific request failure or unexpected behavior and I have a trace showing the span waterfall,
**I want to** see the application logs emitted during that trace automatically correlated by trace_id and span_id,
**so I can** read the narrative of what the code was doing (log messages) alongside the structural view of how long each operation took (spans) -- without manually correlating timestamps or request IDs across separate log files.

### Functional Job
View application logs (info, warn, error, debug) filtered by a specific trace ID, with each log record linked to the span that was active when it was emitted. Logs and traces flow through the same OTEL pipeline and can be queried together in a single backend.

### Emotional Job
Feel that debugging is seamless -- traces show the structure, logs tell the story. Replace the frustration of "I can see the span failed but I do not know what the code was doing at that point" with complete narrative visibility.

### Social Job
Demonstrate operational maturity where observability is unified, not fragmented across separate logging and tracing systems that require manual correlation.

### Outcome Statements
- Minimize the time it takes to find the relevant log entries for a specific traced request
- Minimize the number of tools needed to view both traces and logs for an incident
- Maximize the confidence that all log output from a request is captured and correlated with its trace

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

Otherwise, check each category:

2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

content.text:
# NW-DESIGN: Architecture Design

**Wave**: DESIGN (wave 3 of 6) | **Agents**: Morgan (nw-solution-architect) | **Command**: `*design-architecture`

## Overview

Execute DESIGN wave through discovery-driven architecture design. Morgan asks about business drivers and constraints first, then recommends architecture that fits. Analyzes existing codebase, evaluates open-source alternatives, produces C4 diagrams (Mermaid) as mandatory output.

## Context Files Required

- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/requirements.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/user-stories.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/domain-model.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/ux-journey.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-job-stories.md — Job stories (JTBD Phase 1)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-four-forces.md — Four Forces analysis
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-opportunity-scores.md — Opportunity scores (if multiple jobs)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/constraints.md — Technical and business constraints

## Discovery Flow

Architecture decisions driven by quality attributes, not pattern shopping:

### Step 1: Understand the Problem
Review JTBD artifacts from DISCUSS to understand which jobs the architecture must serve. Morgan asks: What are we building? For whom? Which quality attributes matter most? (scalability|maintainability|testability|time-to-market|fault tolerance|auditability)

### Step 2: Understand Constraints
Morgan asks: Team size/experience? Timeline? Existing systems to integrate? Regulatory requirements? Operational maturity (CI/CD, monitoring)?

### Step 3: Team Structure (Conway's Law)
Morgan asks: How many teams? Communication patterns? Does proposed architecture match org chart?

### Step 3.5: Development Paradigm Selection

Morgan identifies primary language(s) from constraints, then applies:

- **FP-native** (Haskell|F#|Scala|Clojure|Elixir): recommend Functional
- **OOP-native** (Java|C#|Go): recommend OOP
- **Multi-paradigm** (TypeScript|Kotlin|Python|Rust|Swift): present both, let user choose based on team experience and domain fit

After confirmation, ask user permission to write paradigm to project CLAUDE.md:
- FP: `This project follows the **functional programming** paradigm. Use @nw-functional-software-crafter for implementation.`
- OOP: `This project follows the **object-oriented** paradigm. Use @nw-software-crafter for implementation.`

Default if user declines/unsure: OOP. User can override later.

### Step 4: Recommend Architecture Based on Drivers
Recommend based on quality attribute priorities|constraints|paradigm from Steps 1-3.5. Default: modular monolith with dependency inversion (ports-and-adapters). Overrides require evidence.

If functional paradigm selected, Morgan adapts architectural strategy:
- Types-first design: define algebraic data types and domain models before components
- Composition pipelines: data flows through transformation chains, not method dispatch
- Pure core / effect shell: domain logic is pure, IO lives at boundaries (adapters are functions)
- Effect boundaries replace port interfaces: function signatures serve as ports
- Immutable state: state changes produce new values, no mutation in the domain
These are strategic guidance items for the architecture document — no code snippets.

### Step 5: Advanced Architecture Stress Analysis (HIDDEN -- `--residuality` flag only)
When activated: apply complexity-science-based stress analysis — stressors|attractors|residues|incidence matrix|resilience modifications. See `stress-analysis` skill.
When not activated: skip entirely, do not mention.

### Step 6: Produce Deliverables
- Architecture document with component boundaries|tech stack|integration patterns
- C4 System Context diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Container diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Component diagrams (Mermaid) -- only for complex subsystems
- ADRs for significant decisions

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the architect agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`reviewer_model`**: If design review is performed, use this model for the reviewer agent. If `"skip"`, skip design review.
- **`review_enabled`**: If `false`, skip post-design review step.

## Agent Invocation

@nw-solution-architect

Execute \*design-architecture for {feature-name}.

Context files: see Context Files Required above.

**Configuration:**
- model: rigor.agent_model (omit if "inherit")
- interactive: moderate
- output_format: markdown
- diagram_format: mermaid (C4)
- stress_analysis: {true if --residuality flag, false otherwise}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Business drivers and constraints gathered before architecture selection
- [ ] Existing system analyzed before design (codebase search performed)
- [ ] Integration points with existing components documented
- [ ] Reuse vs. new component decisions justified
- [ ] Architecture supports all business requirements
- [ ] Technology stack selected with clear rationale
- [ ] Development paradigm selected and (optionally) written to project CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Component boundaries defined with dependency-inversion compliance
- [ ] C4 System Context + Container diagrams produced (Mermaid)
- [ ] ADRs written with alternatives considered
- [ ] Handoff accepted by nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)
**Deliverables**: See Morgan's handoff package specification in agent file

## Expected Outputs

```
docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/
  architecture-design.md       (includes C4 diagrams in Mermaid)
  technology-stack.md
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
CLAUDE.md (project root)   (optional: ## Development Paradigm section)
```

input.prompt:
Design the architecture for the "OpenTelemetry Observability Migration" feature in /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/jerusalem.

## DISCUSS Wave Artifacts (read these first)

- `docs/requirements/opentelemetry-observability/requirements.md`
- `docs/requirements/opentelemetry-observability/user-stories.md`
- `docs/requirements/opentelemetry-observability/acceptance-criteria.md`
- `docs/ux/opentelemetry-observability/jtbd-job-stories.md`
- `docs/ux/opentelemetry-observability/jtbd-four-forces.md`

## Key Codebase Files to Read

- `app/src/server/logging.ts` — Current Pino logger
- `app/src/server/http/observability.ts` — logInfo/logWarn/logError/logDebug wrappers
- `app/src/server/http/request-logging.ts` — withRequestLogging HTTP wrapper
- `app/src/server/request-context.ts` — AsyncLocalStorage request context
- `app/src/server/runtime/dependencies.ts` — Model initialization + devtools middleware
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` — Server bootstrap
- `app/src/server/runtime/config.ts` — Env config
- `app/src/server/chat/handler.ts` — streamText usage
- `app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts` — generateObject usage
- `package.json` — Current dependencies

## Decisions Already Made

1. **Drop Pino entirely** — Replace with OTEL Logs API for all application logging
2. **OTEL for all three signals**: traces, metrics, logs
3. **Enable AI SDK `experimental_telemetry`** on all generateText/generateObject/streamText calls
4. **Exporters**: Console in dev, OTLP in prod, via standard OTEL env vars
5. **SurrealDB graph traces stay** — separate concern from OTEL, not replaced
6. **Development paradigm**: Functional (already set in CLAUDE.md)
7. **Next ADR number**: ADR-053

## Architecture Constraints

- Runtime: Bun (not Node.js) — verify OTEL SDK compatibility with Bun
- TypeScript, functional paradigm
- No backwards compatibility needed (per project conventions)
- Server is a single Bun.serve process (modular monolith)
- AI SDK v6.0.101 already in use
- 7 model instances (chat, extraction, PM, analytics, embedding, observer, scorer)
- ~20 generateObject call sites, 2 streamText, 2 ToolLoopAgent
- AsyncLocalStorage already used for request context — OTEL context propagation should leverage or replace this

## What I Need From You

### Step 1-3: Already answered
- **Quality attributes**: Debuggability, operational visibility, cost transparency, minimal performance overhead
- **Constraints**: Single developer, Bun runtime, functional TS, no team boundaries
- **Conway's Law**: N/A — single developer

### Step 4: Recommend Architecture
Design how OTEL integrates into the existing modular monolith. Key questions:
- Where does the OTEL SDK bootstrap live? (likely `runtime/`)
- How do the 3 providers (TracerProvider, MeterProvider, LoggerProvider) get initialized?
- How does `experimental_telemetry` get passed to all AI SDK calls? (helper/config pattern)
- What replaces `observability.ts` logInfo/logWarn/logError?
- What replaces `withRequestLogging`?
- How do custom spans wrap business logic (extraction pipeline, observer scan, chat processing)?
- What metrics get defined and where?

### Step 6: Produce Deliverables

Write these files:

1. `docs/design/opentelemetry-observability/architecture-design.md` — Full architecture doc with C4 System Context + Container diagrams (Mermaid). Include:
   - OTEL SDK bootstrap design
   - Telemetry configuration helper for AI SDK
   - Log, trace, and metrics instrumentation patterns
   - Migration path from Pino
   - Integration with existing request context

2. `docs/design/opentelemetry-observability/component-boundaries.md` — Component breakdown:
   - `runtime/telemetry.ts` — SDK bootstrap (providers, exporters, resource)
   - `http/instrumentation.ts` — HTTP request/response tracing (replaces withRequestLogging + observability.ts)
   - `telemetry/logger.ts` — OTEL log helper (replaces Pino wrappers)
   - `telemetry/metrics.ts` — Metric instruments
   - `telemetry/ai-telemetry.ts` — AI SDK experimental_telemetry config factory
   - Any other components needed

3. `docs/adrs/ADR-053-otel-over-pino.md` — ADR for replacing Pino with OpenTelemetry. Alternatives: keep Pino + add OTEL, Pino with OTEL bridge, drop Pino for OTEL-only. Decision: OTEL-only.

Keep the architecture practical and minimal — this is a single-developer project. No over-engineering. Focus on what files change, what new files are created, and how the pieces connect.
